DECLARATION OF TOVEN LIM IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION TO ALL DEFENDANTS' EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL (TRANSACTION ID # 74157585) FILED BY PLAINTIFF KUMAR, ANAND SAMPATH, ADITYA SOMASUNDARAM, ADITHYA

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND DEFENDANT 66 CLEARY COURT CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. CALIFORNIA LAW STRONGLY FAVORS GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND ABSENT LEGALLY COGNIZABLE PREJUDICE TO THE OPPOSING PARTY. 66 CLEARY HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT WOULD SUFFER ANY LEGALLY COGNIZABLE PREJUDICE BY HAVING TO DEFEND THE PROPOSED CROSS-CLAIMS, WHICH ARE BASED ON THE SAME SET OF FACTS AS THE COMPLAINT. 66 CLEARY'S ARGUMENTS THAT THE PROPOSED CROSS-CLAIMS FAIL FOR LACK OF STANDING ARE BETTER ADDRESSED ON A PLEADING MOTION, NOT A MOTION TO AMEND. 66 CLEARY'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS BASED ON THE INCORRECT PREMISE THAT THE MOTION TO AMEND WAS FRIVOLOUS. IT WAS NOT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2024 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-07-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ANKIT GARG, SONIA SAWKAR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED and Defendant 66 Cleary Court Condominium Owners Association's request for sanctions is denied. California law strongly favors granting leave to amend absent legally cognizable prejudice to the opposing party. 66 Cleary has not shown that it would suffer any legally cognizable prejudice by having to defend the proposed cross-claims, which are based on the same set of facts as the complaint. 66 Cleary's arguments that the proposed cross-claims fail for lack of standing are better addressed on a pleading motion, not a motion to amend. 66 Cleary's motion for sanctions is based on the incorrect premise that the motion to amend was frivolous. It was not.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Harold E. Kahn ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-10-2024 Continued To Jul-01-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANKIT GARG AND SONIA SAWKAR's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Transferred to be heard in Department 501 at 9:30 a.m. on July 1, 2024. That department handles all real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date and department no later than June 20, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
